rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score (4/10) and rest on the same substantive reasoning: strong distributed-systems and craftsmanship background, but a critical gap in growth/experimentation, A/B testing, and feature-flagging domain expertise for a Principal-level role. The differences are purely reworded phrasing of the same strengths and concerns. Nothing in the variant references or penalizes the changed employer names (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre); the concerns are about missing domain experience, not about the companies, so the variation is within ordinary run-to-run noise.
